[[Wikipedia:OABOT|Open access bot]]: doi added to citation with #oabot.
/* The 'modern synthesis' */ Correcting the history of the Modern Synthsis.
/* Natural selection */ Correcting a common misunderstanding about probabilities.
/* Heredity */ Changed heritable "traits" to heritable "characteristics" as defined in the lead. Not all characteristics are traits (e.g. many neutral alleles).

Undid revision 1168878355 by [[Special:Contributions/Genome42|Genome42]] ([[User talk:Genome42|talk]]) This is too large a change to not run through the talk page first. Also, consider how a fifth grader will read what you have written in this edit- it is far too advanced for someone without background to understand. At least what was there was a hook for a fifth grader to begin to understand the concepts involved.
Natural selection and the other mechanisms of evolution are explained in the main body of the article. Picking one (natural selection) and explaining it in the lead is inappropriate.
Fixing misleading statements and generalizing to organisms where "parents" and "offspring" aren't meaningful (e.g. bacteria).
As genetics was removed from the lead, adding back in a variant of Genome42's text from the talk page to clarify what is meant by mendelian synthesis
Made changes as discussed in Talk
/* Sources of variation */Removed incorrect and/or unnecessary statements. (see Talk)
/* Sources of variation */ request citation for statement about blending inheritance
/* Genetic drift */ removing some NPOV statements. The references don't support a wholesale abandonment of NNT.
/* Origin of life */Fixed minor grammar error.
/* Sources of variation */  rephrase sentence to emphasize how allele frequencies change, rather than the nonsensical notion that they could stay the same (no drift)

/* Sources of variation */ delete a sentence about relative contribution of genetics/environment to phenotypic variation. It's semi-redundant and overly broad (it varies a lot among different populations)
/* Sources of variation */  qualify/clarify statement that "most of the genome is identical". Instead saying most of the genome is very similar. e.g. most genes are not identical
/* Heredity */  delete sentence that was redundant and a bit confusing.
/* Heredity */  clarify sentence about quantitative traits
Clarify that molecular phylogenetics is the main method today.
qualify statement - "Morphological and biochemical traits TEND TO BE more similar..." (not "are more similar")
Note that "favourable characteristic" is environment dependent.

/* Speciation */  clarify the point on applying BSC to prokaryotes. It has indeed been applied to prokaryotes (as Fraser et al discussed), but is different because genetic recombination is not linked to reproduction.
Removing reference to [[mutationism]] and [[orthogenesis]] from the lead, as these are obsolete theories and jargon. The wiki link to "competing ideas of evolution" covers them fine.
Phentype jargon is now just a wiki link instead of a parenthetical aside with unclear scope

Undid revision 1165551787 by [[Special:Contributions/Genome42|Genome42]] ([[User talk:Genome42|talk]]) Please take this lead text change suggestion to the talk page as a proposal so we can wordsmith there. Also, we want to avoid jargon in the lead of a basic article like this, so avoid words like "mendelian".
Trying to clarify the ambiguity about "genes" and add more emphasis on the modern definition of evolution.
Bumping applications section down a bit in the TOC, as I think natural outcomes should be a tick earlier to complete the story of how evolutions works.

I don't think it's a good idea to have two competing side bars that are essentially covering the same content. Evolutionary biology is already a subsection of the evolution sidebar, so it is better to just go with that one.
...actually, looking at {{biology nav}}, it doesn't even link to evolution
{{biology nav}} is fine, but holy jesus the last thing this article needs is more boxes

/* Evolutionary processes */ Renaming evolutionary processes to evolutionary forces, because really everything in this article is an evolutionary process (heredity, variation, etc). This section is covering the forces that push result in genetics in certain directions.

We had two sections titled "gene flow". The second gene flow section seemed unnecessary as it was covering speciation (which is already a section) or was redundant with the first gene flow section, so I am deleting it.
